title: MCP server security + compliance feedback: /mcp bearer auth + append-intent compliance return (Rec#4 + Rec#5)
description: Close two symmetrical security and feedback gaps in the MCP server: (1) extend bearerAuth middleware to cover the /mcp endpoint (currently only /api and /events are protected), and (2) surface the compliance result already computed by audit-log back to the calling agent via fab_append_intent response. Together these ensure the server auth surface is uniform and agents can self-correct based on compliance feedback.

changes: Add app.use('/mcp', bearerAuth) alongside existing /api and /events registrations
Ensure conditional: only apply when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN is set (matching existing pattern)
Change return type to include compliance result: { compliant: boolean, matched_get_rules_ts: string | null, window_ms: number }
Return the already-computed compliance data instead of void/discarding it
Capture compliance result from appendEditIntentAuditEvents call
Include compliance in the return value: { success, timestamp, entry, compliance }
Pass compliance from service result into tool response payload
Response includes: { success, timestamp, entry, compliance: { compliant, matched_get_rules_ts, window_ms } }

implementation: Read http.ts lines 125-145 to understand bearerAuth conditional pattern; add /mcp registration in same block
Read audit-log.ts appendEditIntentAuditEvents (lines 52-88) to identify where compliance is computed and what shape it has
Modify audit-log.ts to return compliance data from appendEditIntentAuditEvents
Modify append-intent.ts service to propagate compliance in its return object
Modify tools/append-intent.ts handler to include compliance in response JSON
Write integration test: call /mcp without token → expect 401; with token → expect 200

reference.pattern: Existing bearerAuth conditional middleware pattern in http.ts
reference.examples: Mirror the /api and /events bearerAuth registration pattern exactly for /mcp

rationale.chosen_approach: Group auth fix + compliance return as one task — both are server package changes with no cross-package impact
alternatives_considered: Separate tasks for auth and compliance (both are 15-20 min changes; combined is more efficient)
Return compliance as a separate MCP notification (more complex; direct return is simpler and sufficient)
decision_factors: /mcp auth is a 2-line change; grouping avoids overhead of a separate task file
Compliance data is already computed in audit-log.ts — no algorithmic change, only surfacing existing data
rationale.tradeoffs: None significant; both changes are purely additive

risks.description: Adding /mcp bearer auth may break existing stdio MCP connections that don't send Authorization headers
risks.mitigation: Verify bearerAuth applies only when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN env var is set; stdio transport bypasses HTTP auth
risks.fallback: Gate /mcp auth behind a separate FABRIC_MCP_AUTH env var to allow fine-grained control

criteria: HTTP request to /mcp without Authorization header returns 401 when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN is set
HTTP request to /mcp with valid Bearer token returns 200
fab_append_intent response includes compliance object: { compliant: boolean, matched_get_rules_ts, window_ms }
audit-log.ts appendEditIntentAuditEvents has non-void return type
convergence.verification: curl -X POST http://localhost:PORT/mcp (without auth) → 401; with -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' → 200; call fab_append_intent via MCP and check response shape
convergence.definition_of_done: The /mcp endpoint is protected at the same level as /api and /events; agents learn their compliance status from every fab_append_intent call

unit: appendEditIntentAuditEvents: returns compliance object with correct fields
appendIntent service: returned object includes compliance field
integration: POST /mcp without token when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN set → 401
POST /mcp with valid token → forwards to MCP handler
fab_append_intent tool response includes compliance.compliant boolean
manual_checks: Set FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN=test; call fab_append_intent via stdio (should not require HTTP auth — stdio bypasses)
Call via HTTP /mcp without token — confirm 401
success_metrics: 0 unprotected /mcp HTTP requests succeed when auth token is configured
100% of fab_append_intent responses include compliance field

commit.message_template: fix(server): /mcp bearer auth coverage + fab_append_intent compliance feedback return
